babyDevelopment: The baby's skin is very thin and transparent. Fine blood vessels are visible. Sucking and swallowing motions are being practiced. The skeleton is continuing to harden (ossification) from cartilage to bone.
bodyChanges: You might experience nasal congestion due to increased blood flow to mucous membranes. Some women notice 'round ligament pain'—sharp, short pains in the lower abdomen or groin as the uterus grows.
tips: Use a saline spray for nasal congestion
Invest in a good supportive pillow for sleep
Eat small, frequent meals to avoid heartburn

do: Move slowly when changing positions to avoid round ligament pain
Incorporate calcium and Vitamin D into your diet
dont: Overexert yourself with new, high-impact workouts
Ignore any signs of dental problems

travelAdvisory.note: Keep moving during long flights or car rides.

warningSignsToWatch: Severe round ligament pain that persists
Sudden, large amount of vaginal discharge
milestoneDescription: Soft cartilage is slowly turning into hard bone throughout the baby's body.
